bead.title: repo-structure: extract sticky route leases and route-status scheduler state
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Sticky route leases, route-status override filtering, and refresh scheduling remain coupled to root service implementation files instead of living behind internal route-health/status mechanics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` places concrete routing/status mechanics under internal packages. `service.go:18-39` keeps `ResolveRoute` and `RouteStatus` as the public facade boundary. Lease and scheduler implementation remains in `service_route_leases.go:1`, `service_refresh_scheduler.go:1`, and `service_routestatus.go:1`.

PROPOSED FIX
Move sticky lease state, lease matching/expiry, route-status override filtering, and refresh scheduler state into an internal implementation package, preferably `internal/routehealth` unless existing local structure indicates a narrower internal status helper. Keep root methods as adapters preserving public route-status vocabulary.

NON-SCOPE
Do not extract provider/model listing surfaces (`ListModels`, `ListProviders`, `HealthCheck`) except for adapter touch needed to keep compilation green. Do not refresh website docs or changelog in this slice beyond incidental references needed by tests.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / DEPENDENCY
Governing artifact: ADR-008 (`spec:ADR-008`). Parent bead/dependency: `fizeau-cd9af16b`.
bead.acceptance:
1. `service_route_leases.go`, `service_refresh_scheduler.go`, and route-status lease/override code no longer own sticky lease or scheduler mechanics; root code delegates to internal implementation while public CONTRACT-003 route/status types remain at root.
2. Public behavior and JSON shape for sticky route decisions, lease reuse/expiry, route-status override timestamps, and route-status filtering remain unchanged.
3. Focused sticky lease tests pass: `go test . -run 'TestResolveRouteStickyLease.*' -count=1`.
4. Focused CLI status test passes: `go test ./agentcli -run 'TestRouteStatusOverridesSinceFilter' -count=1`.
5. Internal route-health tests pass: `go test ./internal/routehealth/... -count=1`.
6. Full repository gate passes: `go test ./... -count=1`.
7. Hook gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"atomic scope within repo sizing guidance: The bead is clear and testable, but it is not atomic under this repo's sizing rules. It combines root-facade refactoring, internal route-health extraction, route-status override filtering, refresh scheduler state movement, CLI verification, and repo-wide gates in one slice. AGENTS.md explicitly treats cross-layer beads of this shape as too broad and recommends splitting them into dependency-chained sub-beads before claim.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}
